backend/main.mo: Empty Motoko actor serving as the backend entry point with no implemented functionality.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping implemented features (hero section, ingredient search, recipe cards, analytics) to their source files, components, and test IDs with confidence ratings.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence descriptions of each frontend source file, serving as a reference summary for the project's frontend architecture.
frontend/index.css: Base Tailwind CSS file defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens including background, foreground, card, chart, and sidebar variables.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML shell for the SpiceAI React app, mounting the app into a root div and loading the main TypeScript entry point as an ES module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component managing view state (list vs. detail), ingredient search, nutrition stats collection, and composing all major sections (Navbar, Hero, Search, RecipeGrid/Detail, Analytics, Footer).
frontend/src/components/AnalyticsSection: Analytics dashboard section rendering a Recharts bar chart for calorie comparison across viewed recipes and a pie chart for macronutrient breakdown of the last viewed recipe, with empty-state handling.
frontend/src/components/HeroSection.tsx: Full-width hero section with a banner image background, warm gradient overlay, SpiceAI branding, CTA buttons for search and analytics, stat counters, and a decorative SVG wave at the bottom.
frontend/src/components/IngredientSearch: A search component that lets users add ingredients as tags (via Enter or comma) and submit them to find Indian recipes, with predefined suggestions and input validation.
frontend/src/components/Navbar.tsx: A fixed navigation bar that becomes opaque on scroll, displays the SpiceAI logo, and provides smooth-scroll links to Search, Recipes, and Analytics sections with a mobile hamburger menu.
frontend/src/components/RecipeCard.tsx: Displays a single recipe as a card with an image, ingredient match percentage bar, used/missing ingredient badges, and a button to view full recipe details.
frontend/src/components/RecipeDetail.tsx: Shows full details for a selected recipe including nutrition stats, cooking steps, and ingredients, fetched via a custom hook, with loading skeletons and error states.
frontend/src/components/RecipeGrid.tsx: Renders a responsive grid of RecipeCard components with loading skeleton placeholders and an empty-state message when no recipes are found after a search.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A custom hook that creates and caches an Internet Computer actor (authenticated or anonymous) using React Query, and invalidates dependent queries whenever the identity or actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Computer Internet Identity authentication, managing login state, identity persistence, and AuthClient lifecycle.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines two React Query hooks: one to search recipes by ingredients via the Spoonacular API and one to fetch detailed recipe information including nutrition.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet importing Poppins/Playfair Display fonts, configuring Tailwind base/components/utilities, and defining a warm Indian spice-themed CSS custom property palette with light and dark modes.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrapping the root App component.
frontend/src/types/spoonacular.ts: TypeScript interfaces for Spoonacular API data shapes including recipes, ingredients, recipe details with instructions and nutrition, and a NutritionStats summary type.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the theme with custom spice-palette colors, Poppins/Playfair fonts, border radii, box shadows, and animation keyframes for the SpiceAI app.
project_state.json: Project state metadata describing the SpiceAI ingredient-based Indian recipe recommendation app, its architecture (Spoonacular API, React, Chart.js), required image assets, and implemented features.
